title: Koeleria micrathera
taxonomy: Koeleria micrathera belongs to the family Poaceae, commonly known as the grass family. It is a type of grass that falls under the genus Koeleria. Within the Poaceae family, Koeleria micrathera is classified as a member of the subfamily Pooideae. The subfamily Pooideae is further divided into several tribes, with Koeleria micrathera being a part of the Koelerieae tribe. This classification places Koeleria micrathera within the larger hierarchy of plant taxonomy.

The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and digestive problems. intro: Koeleria macthera, also known as June grass, is a perennial grass species native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its narrow leaves and delicate, feathery plumes. The health benefits of Koeleria macthera include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, wounds, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Koeleria macthera is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, often in combination with other herbs to treat coughs, bronchitis, and other respiratory ailments.
